About: It is about the arrival of Jesus in Jerusalem a few days before his crucifixion.
It is about the events of the Passion of Jesus, leading to his crucifixion and resurrection.
Affects: It affects the events of the Passion of Jesus.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the four canonical Gospels.
It is associated with the New International Version.
It is associated with the criterion of multiple attestation.
It is associated with the criterion of dissimilarity.
It is associated with the historical Jesus.
It is associated with the raising of Lazarus.
It is associated with the enthronement of Solomon.
It is associated with the Gihon Spring.
It is associated with the traditional festive Hallel.
It is associated with the Feast of Tabernacles.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the narrative of the four canonical Gospels.
HasA: It has a narrative in the four canonical Gospels.
It has discrepancies between the Gospels.
It has a crowd acclaiming Jesus by waving palm branches.
It has a crowd laying cloaks on the ground to honor Jesus.
It has a crowd shouting 'Hosanna!'.
It has a crowd shouting prophecy-related statements.
It has a crowd shouting 'Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord!'.
It has a crowd claiming Jesus is the king of Israel.
It has a crowd laying down small branches of trees.
It has Jesus looking at the city and weeping over it.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by the waving of palm branches.
It is characterized by the laying of cloaks on the ground.
It is characterized by the shouting of 'Hosanna!'.
It is characterized by the shouting of prophecy-related statements.
It is characterized by the shouting of 'Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord!'.
It is characterized by the claim that Jesus is the king of Israel.
It is characterized by the laying down of small branches of trees.
It is characterized by the use of a donkey.
It is characterized by discrepancies between the Gospel accounts.
It is characterized by scholarly debate regarding the size of the crowd.
HeldOn: It was held on Palm Sunday.
Influenced: It influenced the events of the Passion of Jesus.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the Passover festival.
It was influenced by the deliverance of Israel from bondage in Egypt.
It was influenced by the writing practices of antiquity.
It was influenced by the Raising of Lazarus.
It was influenced by Old Testament prophecy.
It was influenced by the claim that Jesus had a hereditary right to the throne of Israel.
It was influenced by the concept of Jesus' coming kingdom being spiritual.
IsA: It is a narrative in the four canonical Gospels.
It is an event celebrated each year by Christians on Palm Sunday.
It is an episode introducing the events of the Passion of Jesus.
It is a triumphal entry into Jerusalem.
KnownAs: It is known as the triumphal entry into Jerusalem.
It is known as Flevit super illam.
KnownFor: It is known for the arrival of Jesus in Jerusalem.
It is known for being celebrated on Palm Sunday.
It is known for Jesus riding a donkey.
